author: Ross van Zyl
text:
INVESTIGATION:
Root cause: app/views/settings/show.html.erb line 95 — no hidden field before notification_hours checkboxes grid, so unchecking all boxes sends no param and Rails never clears the field.
Related files:
  - app/views/settings/show.html.erb (add hidden_field_tag before grid div at line 95)
  - app/models/user.rb (update normalizer at line 23 to reject blank strings before to_i)
Fix:
  1. In the view, insert before the grid div:
       <%= hidden_field_tag "user[notification_hours][]", "" %>
  2. In the model, change:
       normalizes :notification_hours, with: ->(hours) { Array(hours).map(&:to_i) }
     to:
       normalizes :notification_hours, with: ->(hours) { Array(hours).reject(&:blank?).map(&:to_i) }
Gotchas: Without rejecting blanks in the normalizer, the hidden field's empty string gets cast to 0 (midnight), which would incorrectly add midnight as a notification hour.

author: Ross van Zyl
text: LEARNED: Rails empty checkbox bug requires two fixes: (1) hidden_field_tag with empty value before the checkbox group so the param is always submitted, and (2) .reject(&:blank?) in the normalizer before .map(&:to_i) to prevent the empty string sentinel from being cast to 0 (midnight). Without the reject, unchecking all boxes silently adds midnight as a notification hour.

author: Ross van Zyl
text: Completed: Added hidden_field_tag sentinel and updated normalizer to reject blanks. All 23 user model tests pass.
